title: WOS Studio (Authoring) — Common $defs Library
description: Shared $defs library for the Studio Stage-3 schema family. Carries the StudioMetadataEnvelope (the cross-cutting envelope every Studio object inherits) and the four canonical enums that govern authoring discipline: OriginClass (where this object came from), MappingState (how it relates to the WOS substrate), ReviewState, and LifecycleState. Also carries small reusable shapes (AuthorityGrantApplied, ProvenanceRecordRef, CitationRef). No document marker; this schema is registered by $id only and consumed by every other studio schema via $ref. See studio-authoring/CONCEPT-MODEL.md §6.1 (composition strategy) for the design.
$comment: Promotion status (Stage-3, 2026-05-01): MVP shapes; field lists are illustrative, not exhaustive. Inner fields may evolve as Stage-3 hardens; structural conformance to envelope/originClass/mappingState is load-bearing now.

StudioMetadataEnvelope.description: Cross-cutting envelope every Studio object carries. Defines the load-bearing identity, provenance, lifecycle, and substrate-relationship fields. Per CM §6.1, every Stage-3 schema's top-level shape is `{...envelope fields..., body: <kind-specific>}` — i.e., this $def's properties are SPREAD into the host schema, not nested under an `envelope` key. Schemas that consume this typically use `allOf` to inherit envelope properties + add a `body` property. The two `wosTarget` / `wosShapeRef` fields are present only on schemas whose objects can project to WOS (see CM §6.1 corrected pattern, 2026-05-01).

id.description: Stable identifier within the workspace. SHOULD be a UUID or workspace-namespaced URN; MUST be unique within the workspace per object-kind.

workspaceId.description: Workspace this object belongs to. Workspace ownership is load-bearing for permission resolution and citation scope.

version.description: Monotonic version. Strings allow SemVer (e.g., '1.0.0'); integers allow ordinal versioning. Either is acceptable; a workspace SHOULD pick one convention and stick with it.

parentVersion.description: Prior version (when this object supersedes a previous version). Null on first version.

title.description: Short human-readable label. Used in reviewer UIs and audit summaries.

description.description: Longer human-readable description. Optional; used for context in review.

citations.description: SourceCitation references that ground this object. Per source-vault.md every approved object MUST carry at least one citation OR be backed by an Assumption.

provenance.description: AuthoringProvenanceRecord references. Append-only audit trail for this object. Per authoring-provenance.md every state transition emits a record.

originClass.description: Where this object came from (source-extracted, reviewer-interpreted, locally-asserted, assumed, runtime-observed). Every approved PolicyObject MUST carry exactly one originClass.

lifecycleState.description: Lifecycle state per the entity's lifecycle state machine. Specific allowed values vary by entity (PolicyObject, WorkflowIntent, Scenario, etc. each have their own lifecycle); the host schema constrains values via enum where possible. See per-entity lifecycle in studio-authoring/CONCEPT-MODEL.md §2.

reviewState.description: Optional reviewer-facing review state (distinct from lifecycleState). Used when the host entity has a candidate→approved review flow distinct from publication lifecycle (notably ExtractedClaim and WorkflowElement).

mappingRef.description: Reference to the StudioToWosMapping record for this object (when one exists). Required for objects whose mappingState is `mapsToWos` or `requiresSpecExtension`.

mappingState.description: How this object relates to the WOS substrate. Load-bearing for compilation: `mapsToWos` projects to a WOS path; `authoringOnly` stays in workspace; `requiresSpecExtension` queues a parent-side extension; `unmappedButApproved` is the reviewed escape hatch (rare and noisy).

wosTarget.description: JSONPath naming the projection target in `wos-workflow.schema.json` (when mappingState is `mapsToWos`). Per CM §6.1 corrected pattern (2026-05-01): documents the projection without forcing structural inheritance via $ref.

wosShapeRef.description: Citation of the closest WOS $def or block path for this projection. May be a $ref-style URL (`https://wos-spec.org/schemas/wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/AppealMechanism`) when an authoritative $def exists, or a prose path (`AdverseDecisionPolicy.notice fields`) when the WOS shape lives flat inside a larger block. Documentation only; not validated structurally.

canonicalTermRef.description: Canonical term IRI from the workspace TerminologyMap (per terminology-and-canonical-vocabulary.md). Resolves cross-workspace identity for DataElement-bearing objects.

effectivenessRef.description: Reference to an Effectiveness object (per effectiveness-and-applicability.md). Single composable scope (jurisdictions × temporal × appellate state); never copied. Workflow-level effectiveness on WorkflowIntent; element-level on WorkflowElement narrows it.

authorityGrantsApplied.description: AuthorityGrant references consulted when waiving/overriding/approving this object. Audit trail for fine-grained permission decisions.

OriginClass.description: Origin classification per authoring-provenance.md. Every approved PolicyObject + WorkflowIntent element MUST carry exactly one originClass: `source` (lifted directly from a SourceCitation), `approved-interpretation` (reviewer-judged interpretation of source), `local-practice` (workspace-specific operational practice with no upstream source), `assumption` (explicit gap-fill backed by an Assumption PolicyObject), or `runtime-observed` (proposed by a runtime-observation cluster — Phase-4 reserved seam).

MappingState.description: Mapping disposition per studio-to-wos-mapping.md. Precedence: mapsToWos > authoringOnly > requiresSpecExtension > unmappedButApproved. The four states encode the authoring-vocabulary-vs-substrate split (see CM §5 'WOS as canonical substrate').

AuthorityGrantApplied.description: Reference to an AuthorityGrant consulted during a permission decision. Records the grant id + the action it authorized.

grantRef.description: AuthorityGrant id from the workspace policy.

exercisedBy.description: Subject id (per identity-and-attestation.md) who exercised the authority.

CitationRef.description: Reference to a SourceCitation. May be the citation id (string) for compact form, or an inline citation object for cases where the citation is materialized at use site.

ProvenanceRecordRef.description: Reference to an AuthoringProvenanceRecord. May be the record id (string) or an inline record.

Iri.description: Generic IRI (Internationalized Resource Identifier). Used for canonical term references, DPV terms, LegalRuleML terms, etc. Not enum-constrained — open vocabulary.

FelExpression.description: FEL (Forms Expression Language) expression. Per parent CLAUDE.md FEL is the only expression language WOS uses; FEEL/DMN are rejected as substrate. Validated by parent fel-core at compile time.
